div.head: 277. Editorial Note
div.p: On October 6, the North Atlantic Council met for discussions on Cyprus. The Greek Representative informed the Council that his government would participate in a conference on condition that its agenda include discussion of the ultimate solution to the status of Cyprus. The British Representative indicated his government’s willingness to participate in a conference. The Turkish Representative also indicated his government’s willingness to participate but rejected Spaak’s September 30 memorandum outlining terms of reference for the meeting. Polto 885, October 6, reported on the meeting. (Department of State, Central Files, 747C.00/10–658)
